product_updates.title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
product_updates.content: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.

product_updates.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
product_updates.content: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

product_updates.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
product_updates.content: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

product_updates.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
product_updates.content: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

product_updates.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
product_updates.content: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

product_updates.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
product_updates.content: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

product_updates.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
product_updates.content: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

product_updates.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
product_updates.content: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

product_updates.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
product_updates.content: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

product_updates.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
product_updates.content: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
technical_breakthroughs.content: Old and forgotten "shims" Microsoft failed to revoke have made Secure Boot bypasses simple.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window into its models’ …

technical_breakthroughs.title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
technical_breakthroughs.content: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six feet tall and connected to a supply of liquid helium that keeps them only a few degrees above absolute…
